domain: Yemeni mafraj qat-chew session (~4-hour afternoon window bounded by prayer times; held in single shared mafraj room; mid-session Solomon's hour peak focus; full reset between days)
specific_mechanism: External-event-bounded session window with scheduled mid-session high-focus peak; inter-session reset; productive only within bounded window.
llm_application: MAFRAJ-GATE: a per-session attention gate that is HARD-OPEN only between two external-event triggers (e.g., start-of-task token + end-of-task token tagged by retrieval system), with a SCHEDULED ATTENTION-TEMPERATURE PEAK at the geometric midpoint of the session (temperature drops, attention sharpens); attention is COMPLETELY ZEROED outside the gate window; KV-cache is FORCIBLY CLEARED between sessions. Different from sliding-window attention (position-based) and global attention (always-on) — event-bounded with scheduled mid-session sharpening.
novelty_claim: Sliding-window attention (SWA, Longformer, StreamingLLM, SWAT) gates by POSITION. Gated Attention (Qwen NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper) gates by VALUE-PROJECTION non-linearity. PASTA's chunk parallelism + temporal semantic memory + persistent agent memory all gate by content / time bookkeeping. MAFRAJ-GATE differs by EXTERNAL EVENT-TRIGGERED HARD CYCLIC GATE + SCHEDULED MID-SESSION TEMPERATURE PEAK + FORCED INTER-SESSION KV CLEAR. The combination is qualitatively different from any single sliding/static/value-gated mechanism.
